### Post by forrestcupp on 2008-01-28
You're using the Radeon drivers.  Try installing the proprietary drivers from ATI by using the Restricted Drivers Manager.  Then Compiz should work.  You may need to turn it on by going to Preferences/Appearance and turning on the Visual Effects.

---

### Post by ~vel on 2008-01-28

### Post by forrestcupp on 2008-01-29

compiz --replace
``` and tell us what the output is.

desktop:~$ compiz --replace
Checking for Xgl: not present. 
Detected PCI ID for VGA: 01:06.0 0300: 1002:5960 (rev 01) (prog-if 00 [VGA])
Checking for texture_from_pixmap: not present. 
Trying again with indirect rendering:
Checking for texture_from_pixmap: not present. 
aborting and using fallback: /usr/bin/metacity 
```

---

### Post by forrestcupp on 2008-01-30
Here are some things you need to add to your xorg.conf file.  I should have looked more carefully to begin with.

In your Section "Device" for your radeon, add:
```
        Option          "XAANoOffscreenPixmaps"
```
Under Section "ServerLayout", add:
```
        Option          "AIGLX" "true"
```
Then at the end, add:
```
Section "Extensions"
        Option "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection
```

Save it and restart.  Then try turning on your Visual Effects again.  Let me know if it works.

---

### Post by ~vel on 2008-01-30
No dice =/

### Post by Yellow Pasque on 2008-01-30
> **forrestcupp said:**
> You're using the Radeon drivers.  Try installing the proprietary drivers from ATI by using the Restricted Drivers Manager.  Then Compiz should work.  You may need to turn it on by going to Preferences/Appearance and turning on the Visual Effects.
The recent proprietary drivers only support Radeon 9500 and later. You'd have to go back to 8.28 and use XGL to get effects working with that. However, that shouldn't be necessary.

Please post your /usr/bin/compiz file.

---
